text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to managing projects, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything in advance, teams work in short cycles called sprints, which usually last two weeks. Each day, the team holds a brief standup meeting to discuss progress and any obstacles. Before a sprint begins, they review the backlog—a prioritized list of tasks. These tasks are based on user stories, which describe a feature from the user’s perspective. After each sprint, the team reflects and improves, making this an iterative development process.
